title: Venn-Abers-Calibrated OOF Specialist Blend
summary: Your best nodes already cluster tightly, so the next gain may be from stabilizing blend tails rather than changing core features.
rationale: Your best nodes already cluster tightly, so the next gain may be from stabilizing blend tails rather than changing core features. Venn-Abers calibration is a stronger alternative to simple sigmoid scaling when base learners are overconfident or regime-skewed, and can make a meta-blend more robust on rare high-pressure laps.
implementation_hint: Train 2-4 diverse specialists already supported by your stack (e.g. plain GBDT, categorical-heavy model, regulation-feature model, sequence/renewal model). For each fold, fit Venn-Abers on OOF scores only, transform validation/test scores, then blend calibrated probabilities with a simple logit or rank-average meta-model.
expected_effect: Small CV lift or flatter fold variance; more likely to help LB robustness than to create a huge local jump.
risk: AUC is rank-based, so calibration alone may not help unless it improves the ensemble ordering between specialists.
